transcript.text: Hi.
Hi, how you?
I'm good, how are you?
Yeah, all good.
I think Nicholas is also joining.
How's everyone's week going?
Are you having to do five days of work in three?
Yeah.
Yeah.
Five days for me.

It feels like everyone is in that headspace where it's, yeah, like, let's do as many things and check as many boxes as possible before we're out, but also, like, simultaneously a little bit checked out because of the holiday coming up, so it's an interesting balance.
Yeah, yeah, I bet.
Where are you guys spending, um, your Thanksgiving?
I'll just be, yeah.
a toll, yeah.
Hi, Hi.
Hey, Nick.
Okay, hi, how's it going?
Doing well.
Last day of the week over here.
Excited for Thanksgiving.
Yep.
Perfect.
Um.
All right, so I'm going to go ahead and share my screen.
I've already added everyone to the agenda, so you should get it in your inbox.
But I have some good updates for everyone.
So the PMM team has reviewed the artifacts, and they've also, more importantly, provided all the internal documentation that we needed for the product feature matrix.
So they added docs for AdLumen and N-Central.
Those are the two that we were missing.
We got Cove Data Protection a couple of weeks back.
So I was able to complete the product feature matrix.
It's in your Notion base, but I also added it to our agenda.
So the way it looks like it provides mostly like a table overview of each product, and it pulls, it pulled information from all of the documents that were shared with us.
So we have like a breakdown of the before, during.
After ATT&CK Lifecycle Framework, and then it goes into each of the products.
So we start with N-Central, then it goes into Add Lumen MDR.
It brings down, like, different capabilities, differentiators, and then we go into Cove Data Protection.
And this is what we, I was planning to send this over, to email this over to the PMM team so they can kind of, like, do a quick skim and make sure that everything looks okay.
And then we're going to start using that in the content production process.
Looks great.
I'm glad you were able to get all the feedback for the respective products.
Perfect.
Yeah, I think, yeah, we're in a good spot right now.
And I think with the new agentic pipeline.
And in this artifact, I think the product messaging will be on point.
And then for the next step, so we were going to like revise the existing drafts that we delivered.
So these five, we were going to check them against this product feature matrix and try to identify any inconsistencies or any kind of, misleading statements.
And then we would send them back for your review.

And I was thinking about what would be the easiest way to kind of navigate that process in the sense that if you want us to, like we can do an automation in Airtable and we can have the first step be like PMM review and then followed by like editorial review or brand review.
And we could email them.
I don't know how you've been doing it internally if you've been just kind of like emailing these articles to different PMs.
Yeah, that's kind of what we have been doing.
Nick and I were talking about this a little bit yesterday in terms of the process we'd want to take, but I don't think we've got it completely ironed out.
I mean, if we incorporate PMM early on in the process, you're saying that you would either just send these or tag them in the documents appropriately?
Is that the thought?
Yeah, so the idea is we can set up an automation here and then it would send an email.
Right now we just have like a standard one, but it would send an email to like a group of PMMs and tag them for their review.
We could also like tag one of you in.
So just so you you're aware that the drafts are.
And then once they review, they can just tag us in the doc.
Once they're done with their review, we would implement their feedback and then send it back to Elaine or to anybody else who wants to do like an editorial check.
Cool.
Could it theoretically be a different person based on the products that are mentioned?
Like, could the automation be sophisticated enough to know that if Cove is mentioned in this piece of content, only email to Garrett as an example, rather than just pinging everyone with everything?
Yes, we could do that.
We would just have to add like a column here with the exact product that was mentioned.
And I was wondering what we would do if we mentioned like multiple products.
We would tag multiple PMs, I assume.
Yeah, yeah, that would be ideal if, you know, the piece of content spoke to a variety of our products.
Okay, yeah, that works.
We can add those columns and that's possible.
Okay, cool.
Yeah, I think we'll try and figure that out more internally, but I don't see an issue with that if we want PMM to be incorporated early on in the very initial draft portion.
Yeah, yeah.

No, we do this type of approach with other clients and it works smoothly because it's a lot, like sometimes if they have a lot of feedback, then there's no, we would have to do like a double editorial review or to do that first.
So it's easier to get the product feedback first and then follow that with an editorial one.
Okay, that sounds good.
I think we can get started on revising these like existing five drafts.
The editor who is in charge of like creating the content.
And he is U.S.-based, so he's going to be out tomorrow and Friday, but we can get started working on those as soon as possible, probably even today, and then continue when we come back next week.

And then for the blog ideas review, so we did get some feedback from the PMMs on some of the blogs, but it seems like most of their feedback was focused on articles that they don't want us to do, which is totally fine.
So I think, I hope I'm correct in assuming that anything that they didn't leave comment on is good.
I just about to say, I think that no, no feedback is probably, you can assume that it's, it's good to, to go.
Yeah, I would, I would assume the same thing for sure.
Okay.
Okay, perfect.
I am happy to hear that.
Now we did get like some feedback around like how to focus.
Specific topics, but overall, like, most of them got the green light, which is good.
All right, and then let me see.

All right, and then other than that, I think, I don't know, Elaine, if you also, like, now that you've had, like, the PMMs, like, review the drafts, if you also want to go through these ideas and kind of let us know which ones you want us to prioritize, or if you just want us to, like, give you some suggestions for what we think we should go with first.
Yeah, sure.
Yeah, I think I can take a look and see what we can prioritize.
But if you have a list already, can you send that over, maybe I will review that.
Okay, okay, yeah, I can do that.
Yeah.
And now because...
have editing access, you can also change the status, so if you like something, you can just change the status to approve to start.
It shouldn't be, like, you don't have to, like, leave comments anymore.
And also, quick question.
So you said that you are going to go back on the drafts and use the new writing model, right?
So this is still a part of the calibration process.
Yeah, yeah, exactly.
So I think it's, well, the idea is with the drafts that we have right now, my idea was to just specifically target the product sections.
So anywhere where Enable is being mentioned, we will go and, like, fact check those sections against the feature matrix.
I don't know if that's what you had in mind, like, if you wanted us to do, like, additional work on the drafts.
Thank you.
you.
Thank you.
Thank you.
Thank you.
Yeah, I wouldn't personally mind seeing, like, a regeneration of product-specific mentions.

Like, I know that will be, I think fact-checking's good based on new versus old, but I think it would instill faith in the model if, like, we regenerated mentions of Cove and Central and Ed Blumen just to see what the AI model produces with this new matrix.
You know, with the goal of, ultimately, PMM's being happy with the output and them not needing to spend too much time re-running through the content themselves.
So, if it's not trouble, I think it would be great.
And with the topics that are here, yeah, I mean, anything to do with cyber-resilience can speak to all three of those pillars.
So, I think that just, you know, re-utilizing one of those topics to stress-test it, if you will, will be good.
Mm-hmm.
Yeah, yeah, that works.
And correct me if I'm wrong, but I know Pete already added a bunch of comments, so I think we nailed the language and tone down.
It's just the product, how we package the products, right?
So, okay.
Yeah, yeah, exactly.
Yeah, I think that that's totally fine.
I think that that was, that's really the goal.
So we want to, I think we can, I'm not sure if we can.
So in Atlas, we can regenerate an article from scratch, and then that way you can see, okay, how is the model, like, using this new information?

And I think the article would be kind of like, we could use the same outline, but the article would be like a brand new one, in the sense that it wouldn't be used, like, the rest of the copy would not be the same.
But I think that's also kind of fun.
I mean, because it's using the same writing guidelines, so I don't think that it would be, like, a huge difference.
Yeah, no issues with that.
I think it would just be good to compare the before and after to see how it's different.
Okay, all right, that works.
Okay, that's totally fine.

All right, I think those are all the updates that we had, so I'm, like I said, I'm really happy that we were able to knock this out before, like, the end of Phase 1 and before, Thanksgiving holidays, so that, like, we, when we come back, we can, like, you know, resume where we left off and kind of, like, start generating content in earnest and publishing.
I did forward your feedback on the featured image examples to our designer.
I did ask her to kind of come up with some examples that did not include humans that point to things.
So I will send those examples over to you as soon as they're ready, and then we can, like, fold that into the pipeline.
Bye.
And then, yeah, we can, like, provide those images as well.
Great.
Okay.
So could we expect to see, like, the regenerated draft maybe before our next meeting next week?
And on our end, by end of next week, how many topics should we hope to, you know, move forward into that approved for production cycle?
Like, five to ten?
Or, like, what would be ideal at the end of next week?
I, like, ten to fifteen would be ideal.
As many as possible sometimes.
Like, fifteen is, like, a sweet spot.
It gives us runway for three weeks, so it's a good number.
Okay.
Great.
We'll end to do that then.
Okay.
Perfect.
Any other questions?
Thanks for putting this all together.
Is that what we
Got it all wrapped up.
Or at least, you know, the feedback wrapped up.
Yeah, yeah.
No, I'm happy that we were able to do that.
And thanks for your help coordinating everything.
I really appreciate it.
Okay, perfect.
So I think we can wrap up early.
I hope you have fun time off with your family and friends.
And I will see you next week.
And I'll be in touch with the regenerated drafts.
Thanks a lot, Aida.
Likewise.
Bye-bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Finalize the product feature matrix and align on the content review process.](https://fathom.video/share/Cvjx4ET7smSuTANZex6WrXJY1FF8LBx-?tab=summary&timestamp=120.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [The Product Feature Matrix is complete, integrating docs for N-Central, AdLumen, and Cove Data Protection to ensure accurate product messaging.](https://fathom.video/share/Cvjx4ET7smSuTANZex6WrXJY1FF8LBx-?tab=summary&timestamp=129.0)
  - [A new review workflow will send drafts to specific PMMs based on the products mentioned, streamlining feedback and preventing unnecessary reviews.](https://fathom.video/share/Cvjx4ET7smSuTANZex6WrXJY1FF8LBx-?tab=summary&timestamp=378.0)
  - [To validate the new AI pipeline, GrowthX will regenerate 5 existing drafts from scratch using the matrix, creating a clear "before and after" comparison.](https://fathom.video/share/Cvjx4ET7smSuTANZex6WrXJY1FF8LBx-?tab=summary&timestamp=614.0)
  - [N-able will approve 10–15 blog topics by EOW next week to provide a 3-week content runway.](https://fathom.video/share/Cvjx4ET7smSuTANZex6WrXJY1FF8LBx-?tab=summary&timestamp=847.0)

## Topics

### Product Feature Matrix Finalized

  - [The matrix is complete, integrating documentation for all three key products:](https://fathom.video/share/Cvjx4ET7smSuTANZex6WrXJY1FF8LBx-?tab=summary&timestamp=129.0)
      - [N-Central](https://fathom.video/share/Cvjx4ET7smSuTANZex6WrXJY1FF8LBx-?tab=summary&timestamp=186.0)
      - [AdLumen MDR](https://fathom.video/share/Cvjx4ET7smSuTANZex6WrXJY1FF8LBx-?tab=summary&timestamp=186.0)
      - [Cove Data Protection](https://fathom.video/share/Cvjx4ET7smSuTANZex6WrXJY1FF8LBx-?tab=summary&timestamp=196.0)
  - [It maps each product's capabilities and differentiators to the ATT\&CK Lifecycle Framework.](https://fathom.video/share/Cvjx4ET7smSuTANZex6WrXJY1FF8LBx-?tab=summary&timestamp=160.0)
  - [**Purpose:** Serve as the single source of truth for accurate product messaging in all new content.](https://fathom.video/share/Cvjx4ET7smSuTANZex6WrXJY1FF8LBx-?tab=summary&timestamp=237.0)

### New Content Review Workflow

  - [**Problem:** The current email-based review process is inefficient, sending all drafts to all PMMs regardless of product relevance.](https://fathom.video/share/Cvjx4ET7smSuTANZex6WrXJY1FF8LBx-?tab=summary&timestamp=277.0)
  - [**Solution:** An Airtable automation will route drafts to specific PMMs based on the products mentioned.](https://fathom.video/share/Cvjx4ET7smSuTANZex6WrXJY1FF8LBx-?tab=summary&timestamp=378.0)
      - [**Logic:** A draft mentioning Cove → routes to the Cove PMM (e.g., Garrett).](https://fathom.video/share/Cvjx4ET7smSuTANZex6WrXJY1FF8LBx-?tab=summary&timestamp=378.0)
      - [**Multi-Product:** A draft mentioning multiple products → routes to all relevant PMMs.](https://fathom.video/share/Cvjx4ET7smSuTANZex6WrXJY1FF8LBx-?tab=summary&timestamp=378.0)
  - [**Process Flow:** PMM Review → Editorial/Brand Review.](https://fathom.video/share/Cvjx4ET7smSuTANZex6WrXJY1FF8LBx-?tab=summary&timestamp=277.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Get product facts correct first to avoid rework later.](https://fathom.video/share/Cvjx4ET7smSuTANZex6WrXJY1FF8LBx-?tab=summary&timestamp=441.0)

### AI Pipeline Validation

  - [**Goal:** Validate the new "agentic pipeline" by testing its ability to generate accurate product copy from the matrix.](https://fathom.video/share/Cvjx4ET7smSuTANZex6WrXJY1FF8LBx-?tab=summary&timestamp=614.0)
  - [**Method:** Regenerate 5 existing drafts from scratch using the new matrix.](https://fathom.video/share/Cvjx4ET7smSuTANZex6WrXJY1FF8LBx-?tab=summary&timestamp=614.0)
      - [**Rationale:** This creates a clear "before and after" comparison to prove the model's effectiveness and build PMM confidence.](https://fathom.video/share/Cvjx4ET7smSuTANZex6WrXJY1FF8LBx-?tab=summary&timestamp=660.0)
  - [**Scope:** Focus on product-specific sections; general language and tone are already approved.](https://fathom.video/share/Cvjx4ET7smSuTANZex6WrXJY1FF8LBx-?tab=summary&timestamp=722.0)

### Blog Topic Prioritization

  - [PMMs provided feedback on blog ideas, primarily noting topics to avoid.](https://fathom.video/share/Cvjx4ET7smSuTANZex6WrXJY1FF8LBx-?tab=summary&timestamp=495.0)
  - [**Assumption:** Topics without PMM feedback are approved for production.](https://fathom.video/share/Cvjx4ET7smSuTANZex6WrXJY1FF8LBx-?tab=summary&timestamp=495.0)
  - [**Action:** Elaine will review the full list and approve 10–15 topics by EOW next week.](https://fathom.video/share/Cvjx4ET7smSuTANZex6WrXJY1FF8LBx-?tab=summary&timestamp=847.0)
      - [**Rationale:** This provides a 3-week content runway for GrowthX.](https://fathom.video/share/Cvjx4ET7smSuTANZex6WrXJY1FF8LBx-?tab=summary&timestamp=856.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/Cvjx4ET7smSuTANZex6WrXJY1FF8LBx-?tab=summary&timestamp=205.0)
      - [Send the completed Product Feature Matrix to the PMM team for a final skim.](https://fathom.video/share/Cvjx4ET7smSuTANZex6WrXJY1FF8LBx-?tab=summary&timestamp=205.0)
      - [Regenerate 5 existing drafts from scratch using the new matrix.](https://fathom.video/share/Cvjx4ET7smSuTANZex6WrXJY1FF8LBx-?tab=summary&timestamp=614.0)
      - [Share new featured image examples (non-human-pointing) from the designer.](https://fathom.video/share/Cvjx4ET7smSuTANZex6WrXJY1FF8LBx-?tab=summary&timestamp=821.0)
  - [**N-able:**](https://fathom.video/share/Cvjx4ET7smSuTANZex6WrXJY1FF8LBx-?tab=summary&timestamp=847.0)
      - [Approve 10–15 blog topics by EOW next week.](https://fathom.video/share/Cvjx4ET7smSuTANZex6WrXJY1FF8LBx-?tab=summary&timestamp=847.0)
      - [Finalize the internal PMM review process for the new Airtable automation.](https://fathom.video/share/Cvjx4ET7smSuTANZex6WrXJY1FF8LBx-?tab=summary&timestamp=427.0)

action_items.description: Email product feature matrix to N-able PMMs for skim review
Add product-tagging columns in Airtable; update PMM review automation to route by product
Revise 5 existing drafts vs product feature matrix; send to Elaine for editorial
Email Elaine prioritized blog topic list; then Elaine approves 10–15 topics in Notion
Regenerate 1 cyber-resilience article w/ new matrix; send to Andrew/Elaine for before/after
